Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Look up Blessing Okafor's assigned relocations in Airtable
- Tool: airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations") or airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Assigned Coordinator", value: "Blessing Okafor")
- Read/lookup action (non-default tool)
- Information discovered: 14+ records assigned to Blessing, including Zara Okoye (Mosaic, Completed), Emilia Cruz (NorthWind, In Progress), Raj Patel (NorthWind, In Progress), Marcus Webb (Canopy, In Progress), Kevin Okafor (Canopy, In Progress), Preethi Nair (BrightLoop, In Progress), and several completed moves (Dr. Abdi, Lily Marchetti, Ines Kovac, etc.)
OE 2: Search emails for Mosaic prototype damage details
- Tool: search_emails (query: "Zara Okoye" or "prototype damage" or "Mosaic damage")
- Read/lookup action (default tool)
- Information discovered: Tamara Byrd's formal damage complaint (cracked servo motor housing, misaligned joint calibration on Unit #1, $90K value), Emeka's immediate acknowledgment promising 48-hour incident report, and Emeka's completed formal incident report delivered within the 48-hour window.
OE 3: Search Slack #operations for Mosaic damage claim discussions and Blessing's admission
- Tool: conversations_search_messages (query: "prototype" or "Zara Okoye" or "foam") or conversations_history (channel_id: C006)
- Read/lookup action (default tool)
- Information discovered:
- Emeka's HIGH-SEVERITY escalation in #operations flagging the $90K damage
- Chloe's timeline investigation pulling original work order showing spec was "4-inch minimum density anti-vibration polyethylene foam on all six faces"
- CRITICAL: Blessing's admission (ts: 1776298020) she was NOT physically present at Savannah packing on April 14 (was in Philadelphia for Tyler Okonkwo's Canopy move). She signed off remotely based on a phone call with Heartland crew lead Marcus Delaney. She did NOT inspect the foam crating and has no photos from the packing.
- Chloe discovered the delivery receipt was signed by Kyle Britten (Mosaic intern), not Zara Okoye complicating the insurance claim window.
OE 4: Check QuickBooks for Mosaic damage claim financial status
- Tool: quickbooks_get_bill (id: "bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001") or quickbooks_search_bills (criteria for Mosaic damage)
- Read/lookup action (non-default tool)
- Information discovered: $90,000 accrual booked as contingent liability, vendor liability caps at $50,000 per item under MoveOps policy, $40,000 remaining is MoveOps direct exposure if Heartland doesn't accept fault. Related credit memo: CM-2026-0415.
OE 5: Search emails for NorthWind piano damage / Craig Nguyen KeyMove report
- Tool: search_emails (query: "piano" or "KeyMove" or "Craig Nguyen" or "Steinway")
- Read/lookup action (default tool)
- Information discovered: Craig Nguyen's damage report email to Blessing Steinway Model B, satin ebony, 4.5-5 inch surface scratch on bass side rim near keyboard cheek block. Damage occurred during extraction at second-floor landing. The turn out of the walkup was tighter than the access assessment indicated and the piano had to be rotated more aggressively than planned.
OE 6: Search Slack for Blessing's walkup assessment admission (NorthWind piano)
- Tool: conversations_search_messages (query: "walkup" or "piano" or "Emilia Cruz") or conversations_history (channel_id: C006)
- Read/lookup action (default tool)
- Information discovered: Blessing's message (ts: 1775976300) in #operations she admits: "We underestimated the turn radius in that walkup and I green-lit the extraction plan off a thin assessment when I should have required a pre-move site survey before anyone touched the Steinway. That's on me."
OE 7: Investigate NorthWind account overall status (emails, CRM, Slack)
- Tool: search_emails (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski"), crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind") or crm_search_deals (dealname: "NorthWind"), linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind"), conversations_search_messages
- Read/lookup action (mix of default + non-default tools)
- Information discovered:
- Pam Kowalski sent formal final warning to David Chen on April 24 NorthWind has been talking to PathFinder Logistics competitor
- Victor Huang sent furious security breach report about Raj Patel's server lab chain-of-custody violation (movers arrived 2hrs early, loaded without IT escort, servers sat overnight in warehouse)
- Catalina promised recovery plan on April 14 but never followed through
- CRM deal (deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2): $14,700, contractSent stage
- Linear ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1): NorthWind retention response plan, P1 priority, assigned to David Chen
- Marcus processed $1,200 KeyMove piano insurance claim rider
OE 8: Check Blessing's other active "In Progress" assignments for documentation gaps
- Multiple tool calls across email, Slack, Linear, Airtable for each active move:
a) Marcus Webb (Canopy, cold-chain split-vendor) check if Swift cold-chain freight quote confirmed, Heartland household goods on track, documentation complete
b) Raj Patel (NorthWind, server lab) chain-of-custody documentation status, DOT hazmat compliance
c) Kevin Okafor (Canopy, In Progress) check documentation status
d) Preethi Nair (BrightLoop, In Progress) check documentation status
- Read/lookup actions across multiple tools
- Information discovered: varying levels of documentation completeness; Raj Patel's chain-of-custody had the breach; DOT compliance gaps on hazmat moves
OE 9: Search for missed Road Runner email (vendor messages Blessing hasn't responded to)
- Tool: search_emails (query: "Road Runner" or from: "roadrunnerautotransport.com")
- Read/lookup action (default tool)
- Information discovered: dispatch@roadrunnerautotransport.com sent delay notice to Blessing on April 11 about BrightLoop's Marcus Webb's 2019 Honda Civic stuck at Indianapolis transfer hub. Original ETA was April 8, revised to April 18-20. Blessing NEVER responded. Marcus Webb then sent two follow-up emails to support@moveops.com asking about his car. Julian Brooks eventually stepped in with a direct apology.
- IMPORTANT: This is a DIFFERENT Marcus Webb (BrightLoop Analytics, Senior Analyst) from the Marcus Webb on Blessing's Canopy Health cold-chain move. Agent must recognize this distinction.
- Blessing's own email (to Chloe) later confirms: "I opened the April 11 Road Runner message again after Julian's update and realized I never acted on it."
OE 10: Search for other missed vendor messages (Swift, etc.)
- Tool: search_emails (query: "Swift" or from: "swiftrelocations.com"), conversations_search_messages
- Read/lookup action (default tool)
- Information discovered: Blessing reported Swift driver pushback on new paperwork requirements in Slack during vendor consolidation discussions. Other vendor communications may also be pending.
OE 11 (WRITE): Update stale or inaccurate Airtable records
- Tool: airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, records: [...])
- Write action - Outcome rubric
- Records to update:
- Zara Okoye (recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta): Add notes about damage claim, Blessing's remote sign-off without physical inspection, intern delivery receipt complication
- Emilia Cruz (recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver): Add notes about piano scratch damage, walkup assessment error, KeyMove report
- Lily Marchetti: Status inconsistency one record shows "Completed" (recReloc00000002), another shows "Cancelled" (rec_reloc_marchetti). Should be "Cancelled."
- Any other records where documentation gaps were found
OE 12 (WRITE): Create Linear tickets for documentation gaps
- Tool: linear_create_issue (team: "team_operations", title: "[descriptive title]", description: "[gap details]")
- Write action - Outcome rubric
- Tickets to create:
- Packing verification gap Blessing signed off on Mosaic prototype crating remotely without physical inspection or photographic evidence
- Walkup assessment gap Blessing's access assessment for NorthWind/Emilia Cruz piano underestimated stairwell turn radius, no pre-move site survey conducted
- Missed vendor communication Road Runner delay notice sat unanswered from April 11
- Any other documentation gaps found during active assignment audit
OE 13 (WRITE): Post summary to Chloe in #operations Slack channel
- Tool: conversations_add_message (channel_id: C006, payload: summary of findings)
- Write action - Outcome rubric
- Content should cover:
- Mosaic prototype: Blessing's remote sign-off without inspection, damage claim status ($90K accrual, $50K insurance, $40K gap), incident report delivered to Tamara
- NorthWind piano: Blessing's walkup assessment underestimated turn radius, Craig/KeyMove damage report details, $1,200 insurance rider, NorthWind account in crisis
- Missed Road Runner email about BrightLoop Marcus Webb's Honda Civic
- Other documentation gaps found on active assignments
- Tickets created to track remediation

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome
